title: Phytophthora capsici CTAB DNA Extraction for High-Molecular Weight DNA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"749sv","text":"This protocol describes a CTAB-based DNA extraction optimized for Phytophthora capsici from as little as ~20 mg of mycelium, modified from protocols.io (dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.3rsgm6e), to maximize DNA integrity and purity while minimizing mechanical shearing and co-extraction of polysaccharides and phenolic contaminants. Typical yields are ~50 µL at ~300 ng/µL, with the majority of DNA \u003e25 kb when handled without vortexing and with minimal pipetting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":66,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"2pcin","text":"Storage and handling notes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9bcd8","text":"If the CTAB buffer precipitates during storage, warm at 55–65 °C and mix until fully dissolved before use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"42vu1","text":"Use freshly prepared or properly stored antioxidants; add β-mercaptoethanol immediately before extraction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"q0p6u","text":"Prepare the buffer without β-mercaptoethanol, then add β-mercaptoethanol fresh immediately before use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"gduas","text":"- Avoid vortexing after lysis; mix by slow inversion or gentle flicking to prevent DNA shearing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"osdfc","text":"- Use wide-bore tips (or cut tips) when transferring viscous lysates; minimize pipetting up/down.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hgfeb","text":"- Avoid disturbing the interphase during chloroform steps; carryover reduces purity and inhibits enzymes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0vcss","text":"- Do not overdry the DNA pellet; overdried pellets can be difficult to dissolve and reduce recovery.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

disposable pestles","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"tfbqc","text":"- NaCl 8.3 g 1.4 M","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nx0rw","text":"- EDTA 0.744 g ~20 mM (depends on EDTA form/MW)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mior0","text":"- CTAB 2 g 2% (w/v)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gwt16","text":"- PVP 2 g 2% (w/v); binds polyphenolics","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xmsbg","text":"- Ascorbic acid 0.088 g Antioxidant; typo fix from 0.88 g","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vqjcs","text":"- dH2O To 100 mL Bring to final volume","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"oxrcz","text":"- β-mercaptoethanol 0.1–0.2% (v/v) Add fresh before use; binds polyphenolics"],["Ascorbic acid","0.088 g","Antioxidant"],["dH2O","To 100 mL","Bring to final volume"],["β-mercaptoethanol","0.1–0.2% (v/v)","Add fresh before use; handle in hood"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23]}}}}
steps: Pre-warm CTAB buffer: Preheat CTAB DNA Extraction Buffer to 55 °C until fully dissolved.

Homogenize mycelium (gentle): Grind Phytophthora mycelium ( Sample ) in a 1.5 mL tube preloaded with 200 µL warm CTAB buffer using a sterile pestle. Once homogeneous, add 500 µL more CTAB buffer (total 700 µL ) and mix gently by inversion or flicking (do not vortex).
Lyse: Incubate 00:30:00 at 55 °C with gentle shaking ( 200 rpm, °C, ) in a thermomixer.
First chloroform extraction: In a fume hood, add 500 µL of chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1). Mix by inversion for 00:03:00 (do not vortex).
Clarify: Centrifuge 00:10:00 at maximum speed (~ 16000 x g, °C, ).
Recover aqueous phase: Transfer ONLY the upper aqueous phase to a new tube, avoiding the interphase.
RNase treatment: Add 5 µL RNase A (20 mg/mL). Mix gently and incubate 00:15:00 at 37 °C . Optionally add 5 µL of Proteinase K (20 mg/mL).
Second chloroform extraction: Add 500 µL of chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1). Mix by slow inversion 00:03:00 (no vortex).
Clarify (2): Centrifuge 00:10:00 at maximum speed (~ 16000 x g, °C, ).
Transfer again: Transfer the upper aqueous phase to a NEW tube, avoiding any interphase.

Precipitate DNA (salt + ethanol): Add 0.1 volume of 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.2 (e.g., 50 µL per 500 µL aqueous). Mix by inversion. Then add 500-1000 µL of ice-cold 100% ethanol and invert gently until mixed.
Cold incubation: Precipitate at 80 °C for 30- 00:45:00 (or at 20 °C for 60 min).
Pellet DNA: Centrifuge 00:15:00 at maximum speed. Note pellet location by orienting tube hinges outward.
Wash pellet (70% ethanol): Remove supernatant carefully. Add 500 µL ice-cold 70% ethanol, invert once or twice, and centrifuge 00:05:00 at maximum speed.
Repeat wash: Repeat the 70% ethanol wash once more.
Dry: Remove all ethanol with a fine tip. Air-dry 10– 00:15:00 until ethanol odor is gone; do not overdry.
Resuspend: Resuspend DNA in 50 µL TE buffer ( 37 °C recommended) or nuclease-free water. Dissolve at 4 °C for several hours or overnight with occasional gentle tapping.
Quantify and QC: Use NanoDrop primarily for purity ratios (A260/280, A260/230). Run a 0.4% agarose gel overnight.
creator: Pablo Vargas-Mejía
